mappings.rationale: Directly addresses religious rites, rituals of worship, passage rites, and ceremonial practices — the core subject matter of 812.A.
Directly covers pilgrimage as a religious practice across traditions, a specific topic listed in 812.A.1.c.
Directly addresses monasticism as a form of religious life and institution, a specific topic listed in 812.A.3.c.
Covers sainthood across religious traditions, directly relevant to 812.B.3 on sainthood as a religious institution.
Examines martyrdom as a religious practice and institution across traditions, relevant to 812.B.3 on sainthood and 812.A on religious customs.
Covers the Bible as sacred writing, directly relevant to 812.C.1 on sacred writings as material manifestations of religious belief.
Covers the Quran as sacred writing, directly relevant to 812.C.1 on sacred writings.
Addresses the Old Testament as sacred scripture, relevant to 812.C.1 on sacred writings.
Addresses the New Testament as sacred scripture, relevant to 812.C.1 on sacred writings.
Examines the Hebrew Bible as sacred literature, relevant to 812.C.1 on sacred writings.
Covers important sacred religious texts, relevant to 812.C.1 on sacred writings.
Examines non-canonical sacred writings of early Christianity, relevant to 812.C.1.
Covers the Book of Mormon as a sacred text, relevant to 812.C.1 on sacred writings.
Addresses a foundational liturgical text used in worship rituals, relevant to 812.A.1 on rituals of worship and 812.C.3 on ceremonial objects.
Covers religious art, architecture, and iconography in Christianity, directly relevant to 812.C.2 on art and architecture and religious symbolism.
Addresses doctrines including sacramental theology and religious creeds, relevant to 812.A on religious rites and 812.B on religious institutions.
Explores the nature of faith as it relates to religious practice and institutional life, relevant to 812.A on religious customs and 812.B on religious institutions.
Provides an overview of religious practices, institutions, and material culture across traditions, relevant to the broad scope of section 812.
Covers spiritual practices including prayer, meditation, and asceticism, relevant to 812.A.1.a on prayer and 812.A.3.e on asceticism.
Examines fundamentalism's impact on religious institutions and regulation of personal behavior, relevant to 812.A.3 and 812.B.
Covers religious practices and institutions across Judaism, Christianity, and Islam, relevant to the comparative aspects of 812.
Covers anthropological study of religious rituals, passage rites, and social organization, relevant to 812.A.2 on passage rites and 812.B on religious institutions.
Examines religious regulation of speech and behavior regarding the sacred, relevant to 812.A.3 on religious regulation of personal and social behavior.
Explores religious institutions' relationship with warfare including crusades and jihad, relevant to 812.B on religious leaders and institutions.
Addresses religious regulation of marriage practices, relevant to 812.A.2 on marriage rites and 812.A.3 on religious regulation of personal behavior.
Covers religious law as a system for regulating personal and social behavior, directly relevant to 812.A.3.a on religious law.
Covers religious art, architecture, and iconography of ancient Egypt, relevant to 812.C.2 on art and architecture and religious symbolism.
Covers a major religious council and its impact on religious institutions and practices, relevant to 812.B.2 on councils and 812.A on religious rites.
